name: Household Debt-to-Income Ratio

book_quotes.quote: Households, whose real incomes had been squeezed by the slowdown in real wage growth, were offered ever-cheaper debt in order to maintain growth in consumer spending. In consequence, as shown in figure 16.9, the household debt-to-income ratio grew dramatically in the 1980s, which added fuel to the boom.

Figure 16.9 Household Debt-to-Income Ratio, United States, 1975-2011. HHDebt = Household Debt (Flow of Funds TD3, line 2); HHDispPersInc = Household Disposable Personal Income (T2.1, line 27); HHDebtIncRatio = Household Debt/Income Ratio = HHDebt/HHDispPersInc.

primary_source.agency: Federal Reserve Board (debt) and US BEA (income)
primary_source.publication: Federal Reserve Z.1 Flow of Funds (now Financial Accounts of the United States); BEA NIPA Table 2.1
primary_source.table_or_series_id: Numerator: Z.1 Table D.3 line 2 (Household debt); Denominator: BEA NIPA T2.1 line 27 (Disposable Personal Income)
primary_source.available_through: Federal Reserve Z.1 data download; BEA iTable; FRED

formula: HHDebtIncRatio_t = HHDebt_t / HHDispPersInc_t (both in current USD)
components: HHDebt: Federal Reserve Z.1 Table D.3 line 2 (Household sector total debt)
HHDispPersInc: BEA NIPA Table 2.1 line 27 (Disposable Personal Income)

extension_candidates.source: FRED CMDEBT (Households and Nonprofit Organizations; Credit Market Instruments; Liability)
concerns: Fed Z.1 was restructured 2013 (renamed Financial Accounts); pre/post split needs careful splice
CMDEBT excludes nonprofit-only debt vs Shaikh's D.3 line 2 which is household-only — minor level difference

extension_candidates.source: FRED HCCSDODNS (Households; Debt Securities and Loans; Liability)
concerns: Better match to Shaikh's intended D.3 line 2 (household sector excl nonprofits)
Direct continuation; periodic BEA comprehensive revisions cause minor level shifts
CD2 used PI (Personal Income) as proxy in extension splice — wrong concept; we recommend DPI for fidelity to book

extension_candidates.source: FRED TDSP / consumer debt service from Fed
concerns: TDSP is debt SERVICE ratio not debt/income — different concept; documented here only as related family

methodology_notes: Direct two-series ratio; no transformation. Annual values are quarterly Z.1/NIPA series averaged to annual.
Per CD2 (S100), three subseries: S100-A (debt), S100-B (income), S100-C (ratio).
CD2 extension used FRED CMDEBT/PI; PI is Personal Income (broader than DPI) — Shaikh's spec is explicit on Disposable PI (T2.1 line 27). Recommend DPI not PI for Phase 5 to match book exactly.
Note book caption period is '1975-2011' but title at Fig 16.9 also reads 1975-2011 (line 462 of extract); CD2 says 1975-2012 — defer to book caption (1975-2011).
open_questions: CD2 used CMDEBT (household+nonprofit) and PI (broader than DPI) — both are slight wrong-concept proxies. Phase 4 must ratify whether to use HCCSDODNS+DPI for tighter fidelity at cost of code rewrite.
Fed Z.1 D.3 line 2 numbering may have shifted in restructured Financial Accounts — verify current FA table mapping.

review_history.notes: Ported from CD2 S100 with verbatim quotes from p. 735 narrative and Appendix 16.1 p. 899.
